text: In the first space, I feel a quiet sense of anticipation. I am an outsider lingering on the threshold, observing the backs of others as they focus on something ahead. The hum of murmured conversation, the orderly rows, and the sterile lighting evoke both comfort and a slight unease—the formality of gathering, yet my own distance from its center. In the second space, I am enveloped by color, text, and screens—a curated environment declaring its own artificiality. The contrast between reflective observation and immersive spectacle prompts me to question my role: am I participant, observer, or the observed? Both rooms ask me to consider the boundaries between self and world, reality and performance. I am contemplative, quietly aware of my own presence here.
